reasoning: The LLM and programmatic signals converged on several major defects, primarily involving visual content duplication where large image snippets repeat already extracted text. Additionally, programmatic detectors flagged fallback-rendered images and truncated alternative text for three images. With a total of 4 major defects, count-based routing thresholds require this document to be sent to HUMAN_REVIEW.

concerns.analysis: The HTML output contains a visual duplication of the text content. The numbered list on page 1 is fully extracted as standard HTML text (`<ol>`), but a large image snippet of this exact same text is also embedded immediately after the concluding paragraph. The alt text for this image confirms it contains a screenshot of the already-transcribed list requirements.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Numbered list items 3 and 4 on page 1 regarding sediment basin baffle placement and contacting well owners.
concerns.output_quote:
The text is present as structured HTML (`
    ...
  1. Show the sediment basin baffle placement...
    2. ...`) and is duplicated visually by an `img` element with the alt attribute: 'A snippet of an official document containing a numbered list of requirements regarding sediment basin baffles and contacting well'.
concerns.location: Page 1 / HTML body after the concluding paragraph

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (2 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=2. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Degraded image quality: 3 image(s) used fallback rendering instead of direct extraction
concerns.analysis: fallback_rendered_images=3. Examples: p1: "The top portion of an official letterhead from the"; p1: "A snippet of an official document containing a num"; p2: "The closing section of a formal document featuring". These images were not directly extracted from the PDF — instead, an approximate page region was rendered as a PNG. Fallback images may be cropped incorrectly, show the wrong content, or miss meaningful visual elements.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 3 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (3 truncated mid-sentence) — e.g. p1: "...Department of Environmental Quality, featuring the"; p1: "...garding sediment basin baffles and contacting well"; p2: "...faint blue signature above the printed name Judith"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=3 (truncated=3, placeholder=0, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

The source document uses underline formatting for 'Certified Mail' and 'Return Receipt Requested' on Page 1, as well as 'Certified Mail' on Page 2. The extraction JSON converts these to plain text, entirely dropping the underline emphasis which has no markdown equivalent. This is a genuine loss of visual emphasis.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Underlined text: "Certified Mail" and "Return Receipt Requested" (Page 1); "Certified Mail" (Page 2).
concerns.output_quote: Plain text: "Certified Mail\nReturn Receipt Requested" and "Certified Mail"

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (1): North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality | Divisio
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=1. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

action_items: Remove duplicated images that simply repeat text content
Verify and improve image extraction quality where fallback rendering was used
Complete the truncated alt text descriptions for meaningful images
